author: Tertullian

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Less Reprehensible Theories in the Heresy. Bad is the Best of Valentinianism.

ccel.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

title: Less Reprehensible Theories in the Heresy. Bad is the Best of Valentinianism.

source.title: ANF03. Latin Christianity: Its Founder, Tertullian

verses.text: How much more sensible are they who, rejecting all this tiresome nonsense, have refused to believe that any one Æon has descended from another by steps like these, which are really neither more nor less Gemonian; but that on a given signal the eight-fold emanation, of which we have heard, issued all at once from the Father and His Ennœa (Thought),—that it is, in fact, from His mere motion that they gain their designations.
When, as they say, He thought of producing offspring, He on that account gained the name of Father.
After producing, because the issue which He produced was true, He received the name of Truth.
When He wanted Himself to be manifested, He on that account was announced as Man.
Those, moreover, whom He preconceived in His thought when He produced them, were then designated the Church.
As man, He uttered His Word; and so this Word is His first-begotten Son, and to the Word was added Life.
And by this process the first Ogdoad was completed.
However, the whole of this tiresome story is utterly poor and weak.
